title: ancistrachne maidenii
intro: Ancistrachne maidenii, also known as the Maiden's Hair Grass, is a rare and endangered species of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. The plant is native to New Zealand and has been used by the indigenous Maori people for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Ancistrachne maidenii include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds. In traditional Maori medicine, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion, and it can also be used to make a topical ointment or salve for skin conditions.
